page: Amazon Web Services c5d.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8275CL processor running at 3.4 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, translating to 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and a 35 MB L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, providing high-speed local instance store capabilities, and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place the instance in the average tier for most standard operations. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests yielded scores of 1407.48 and 7397.91 respectively, while Geekbench returned a single-core score of 1172.0 and a multi-core score of 4511.0. Memory bandwidth tests showed 438.23 GB/sec for cached read-only small blocks and 51.06 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. However, the instance exhibits weaker performance in specific areas, such as multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and image processing. Large language model inference speeds are also limited, with a 7B model achieving only 11.31 tokens/sec for prompt processing and 6.57 tokens/sec for text generation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c5d.2xlarge offers a specialized resource profile that balances dedicated compute power with fast local NVMe storage. This makes it highly suitable for workloads that require high CPU clock speeds and low-latency scratch space without the overhead of network-attached storage. Appropriate workloads include static web serving, which achieved a throughput of 6.66 GB/sec, Redis database operations, and software compilation tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c5d.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8275CL processor running at 3.4 GHz, it includes a 200 GB local NVMe SSD instance store and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate average performance across standard compute and memory tasks, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 4511.0 and a static web serving throughput of 6.66 GB/sec. However, it shows weaker performance in multi-threaded decompression and LLM inference. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring dedicated CPU power paired with high-speed local scratch space, making it suitable for web serving, Redis databases, and software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5d.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 200 GB local NVMe SSD, delivering average performance for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5d.2xlarge is an 8-vCPU compute-optimized instance featuring 16 GB DDR4 RAM and 200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for intensive data processing.
bullet_points: 8 vCPUs and 16.0 GB DDR4 RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 8275CL processor at 3.4 GHz
200 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
Average performance in static web serving and Redis database operations
Weaker performance in multi-threaded decompression and LLM inference workloads
Suitable for software compilation, web serving, and database applications
